mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market, which drives higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) is consistently negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, higher borrowing costs compress corporate profit margins and increase the discount rate for future earnings, causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment and high inflation signals an overheating economy, forcing the central bank to maintain a restrictive monetary policy. This joint condition negatively impacts equity valuations through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when both trigger conditions are met fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, unexpectedly strong retail sales growth signals persistent aggregate demand, increasing the likelihood of further monetary tightening by the central bank, which negatively impacts equity markets.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS > 1% while 12-month change in CPIAUCSL > 3.5%
failure_condition: The correlation between strong retail sales surprises and subsequent 20-day returns is positive during periods of high inflation (CPIAUCSL y/y > 3.5%).

mechanism_narrative: A negative rate of change in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing and supply-side activity, which precedes lower corporate earnings and leads to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The hit rate (correctly predicting negative returns) of the signal is below 55% over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in housing starts reflects falling consumer confidence and investment in a key economic sector, signaling a broader economic slowdown and negatively impacting forward equity returns.
failure_condition: A regression of 60-day forward returns on the HOUST signal shows a statistically insignificant or positive coefficient (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation and decelerating real GDP growth creates a stagflationary environment. This erodes corporate margins via input costs while simultaneously reducing demand, leading to sharply negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL change > 4% and quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth < 1% annualized
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return during periods matching the trigger condition is not statistically different from the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate, even from a low base, signals a negative inflection point in the labor market. This is a leading indicator of recession and prompts a rapid de-risking from equities.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in UNRATE and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 5-year rolling window.
